text: Define isothermal, adiabatic, and isentropic process of compressible flow.
Derive the energy equation of a compressible flow in terms of temperature and velocity.

A helicopter is travelling at a height of $1500 \text{ m}$, above the ground. Find the pressure of air on it, if the pressure at the ground level is $100 \text{ kPa}$ at $20 ^\circ\text{C}$. Take $R = 0.287 \text{ kJ/kg.K}$.
Define with relationship the controlling parameters in compressible flow.
With neat sketch describe the propagation of elastic pressure wave by an object moving in a compressible fluid.

A normal shock is formed while the air is flowing at $450 \text{ m/s}$ in a diverging section and the supersonic flow changes to subsonic abruptly. Ambient pressure is $1.03 \text{ bar}$, temperature $27 ^\circ\text{C}$, $\gamma = 1.4$, $R = 0.287 \text{ kJ/kg.K}$. Determine (i) the Mach number upstream and downstream of the shock, (ii) the pressure rise across the shock, and (iii) the velocity and temperature after the shock.
Define fan, compressor, and blower in respect of specific ratio and rise in system pressure.
Classify the air compressor according to working, action and number of stages.

A centrifugal blower running at $8000 \text{ rpm}$, compresses $5 \text{ m}^3\text{/s}$ of air from $1 \text{ bar}$ and $15 ^\circ\text{C}$ to $2 \text{ bar}$. The index of compression $n = 1.3$. The velocity of flow is $70 \text{ m/s}$ and is constant throughout the impeller. The impeller diameter at inlet and outlet are $0.3 \text{ m}$ and $0.6 \text{ m}$ respectively. Determine (i) the impeller blade angles at inlet and outlet, and (ii) the impeller width at inlet and outlet.
Describe the working principle an axial flow multistage drum type compressor with neat sketch.
Write short notes on hydraulic torque converter with neat sketch.
What is surge in open channel flow? Describe the working principle of surge tank in hydro-electric power plant with neat sketch.
Describe the constructional details of overshot water wheel.
Describe the working principle of undershot water wheel with neat sketch.

An overshot water wheel has approaching canal $1.5 \text{ m}$ wide. The water flows in the canal with a velocity of $1.5 \text{ m/s}$ and $200 \text{ mm}$ deep. Determine the power available from the water wheel if the water fall is $20 \text{ m}$ and the efficiency of the wheel is $75\%$.
How air vessel in reciprocating pump works to maintain a uniform discharge from the pump. Discuss with neat sketch.

A double acting reciprocating pump has a stroke of $300 \text{ mm}$ and a piston of diameter $150 \text{ mm}$. The delivery and suction heads are $26 \text{ m}$ and $4 \text{ m}$ respectively including friction heads. If the pump is working at $60 \text{ rpm}$, find power required to drive the pump with $80\%$ efficiency.
What is meant by turbomachinery? Derive Euler turbomachinery equation.

A Pelton wheel is required to generate $4000 \text{ kW}$ under an effective head of $400 \text{ m}$. Determine (i) total flow in $\text{m}^3\text{/s}$ and (ii) size of jet. Assume generator efficiency $95\%$, overall efficiency $85\%$ and coefficient of velocity $0.98$ and speed ratio $0.46$.
How the speed of a reaction turbine governed by servomotor, discuss with neat sketch.

A Kaplan turbine operating under a net head of $20 \text{ m}$ develops $100 \text{ kW}$ power with an overall efficiency of $85\%$. The speed ratio is $2$ and flow ratio is $0.6$. The hub diameter of the wheel is $0.35$ times the outside diameter of the wheel. Determine (i) the diameter and (ii) speed of the turbine.
Define compressible fluid and incompressible fluid. Also define three laws of perfect gas with mathematical relationship.

One liter of nitrogen gas at $100^{\circ}C$ is suddenly expanded to 2 liters according to the law $Pv^{1.35} = C$. Find the change in temperature of the gas and work done. ($R=0.29\text{ kJ/kg}^{\circ}\text{K}$).
What is Mach number? Considering the variation of velocity with area in case of compressible fluid flow, show that $\frac{dA}{dV} = \frac{A}{V}(M_a^2 - 1)$.

A supersonic nozzle is to be designed for air flow with Mach number 3 at the exit section which is 20 cm in diameter. The pressure and temperature of air at the nozzle exit are to be $7.85\text{ kN/m}^2$ and 200K respectively. Determine the reservoir pressure and temperature and the throat area. Take $k=1.4$.
State the effect of variation of back pressure of compressible flow in a convergent-divergent nozzle with the help of a diagram.

At a certain section of a duct in which air is flowing at a temperature of $32^{\circ}C$ and pressure of $79.95\text{ kN/m}^2$ with a velocity of 365 m/s. Assuming isentropic flow (reversible adiabatic) determine:
the velocity and temperature at a section where the pressure is $122.63\text{ kN/m}^2$ and
the Mach number at both the sections. Take $R = 287\text{ Nm/kg}^{\circ}\text{K}$ and $k$ for air = 1.4.
Differentiate among fan, blower and compressor in respect of specific ration and pressure rise.
Derive the formula of workdone by reciprocating air compressor with clearance volume and polytropic process.

Estimate the minimum work required to compress 1kg of air from 1 bar $27^{\circ}C$ to 16 bar in two stages, if the law of compression is $Pv^{1.25} = \text{Constant}$ and the intercooling is perfect. Take $R=287\text{ J/kg}^{\circ}\text{K}$.
Describe the working principle of undershot water wheel with net sketch and constructional details.

A breast water wheel of 8 m diameter and 2m width is working under a head of 5m. The depth of shroud is 400mm and the buckets move with a velocity of 1.5 m/s with 5/8 full. Calculate the discharge and power of the wheel, if its efficiency is 60%.
Derive the equation of hydraulic efficiency of an impulse turbine and hence prove that for maximum efficiency wheel velocity should be the half of the jet velocity.

A Pelton wheel having semi-circular buckets and working under a head of 140meters, is running at 600 rpm. The discharge through the nozzle is 500 litres/sec and diameter of the wheel is 600 mm. Find
Power available at the nozzle, and
Hydraulic efficiency of the wheel, if coefficient of velocity is 0.98. Assume $k=1$.
What is the specific speed of a centrifugal pump? Prove that the specific speed is $N_s = \frac{N \sqrt{Q}}{H^{3/4}}$.

A double acting reciprocating pump has a stroke of 300 mm and a piston of diameter 150 mm. The delivery and suction heads are 26m and 4m respectively including friction heads. If the pump is working at 60 rpm, find the actual power required to drive the pump with 80% efficiency.
How surge tank or shaft works in a typical hydroelectric scheme, discuss with net sketch.

Write short notes on (any three):
Hydraulic press
Hydraulic crane
Effect of friction in the suction and delivery pipes on the indicator diagram of reciprocating pump
Draft tube.
Explain the term choking in nozzle flows.
Show that the sonic velocity in an ideal gas depends on the temperature and nature of the gas.

A converging-diverging nozzle is to be designed to expand air isentropically to atmospheric pressure from a large tank in which properties are held constant at $5^\circ C$ and $304\text{ kPa (abs.)}$. The desired flow rate is $1\text{ kg/s}$. determines the exit area of the nozzle.

Air flows in a variable cross-sectional area duct. Derive the relation for velocity change in term of area change $\frac{dA}{A}$ of the duct, assuming one dimensional isentropic flow and hence explain how an accelerating flow can be obtained.

Air flowing with a velocity of $600\text{ m/s}$, a pressure of $60\text{ kPa}$, and a temperature of $260\text{ K}$ undergoes a normal shock. Determine (i) the Mach number before and after the shock, and (ii) the entropy change across the shock.
Prove that the work done by a single stage reciprocating compressor without clearance volume for isentropic compression is $W = m c_p (T_1 - T_2)$, where the symbols have their usual meaning.

Estimate the minimum work required to compress $1.5\text{ kg}$ of air from $1.0\text{ bar, } 27^\circ C$ to $20\text{ bar}$ in two stages, if the law of compression is $pv^{1.25} = \text{constant}$ and intercooling is perfect. Take $R = 287\text{ J/kg-K}$.
What is cavitation in pump? What are its consequences? How can cavitation be avoided?

Show that the maximum acceleration head in a reciprocating pump, without air vessel is given by the relation $H_a = \left( \frac{l}{g} \right) \times \left( \frac{A}{a} \right) \times w^2 r$, where, $l = \text{length of pipe, } A/a = \text{ratio of cylinder area to pipe area and } w = \text{angular velocity of crankshaft}$.

A double acting reciprocating pump has a bore of $175\text{ mm}$ and a stroke of $350\text{ mm}$. The suction pipe has a diameter of $150\text{ mm}$ and is fitted with an air vessel. Determine the crank angle, at which there is no flow of water to or from the vessel. Assume crank speed at $150\text{ rpm}$ and plunger has simple harmonic motion.
Derive Euler’s equation of energy transfer in rotodynamic machines.
Design a Pelton wheel for a head of $150\text{ meters}$ at a speed of $360\text{ rpm}$. Power to be developed by the turbine is $600\text{ kW}$. Assume the overall efficiency of the wheel as $85\%$.
Explain the significance of specific speed of water turbine.
What factors decide whether a Kaplan, Francis or a Pelton type turbine would be used in a hydroelectric project?

A Francis turbine works under a head of $25\text{ m}$ and produces $11800\text{ kW}$ while running at $120\text{ rpm}$. The turbine has been installed at a station where atmospheric pressure is $10\text{ m}$ of water and vapor pressure is $0.2\text{ m}$ of water. Determine (i) specific speed of the turbine, and (ii) the maximum permissible height of the draft tube.
Explain why priming is necessary in a centrifugal pump.
Discuss the various methods adopted to increase the efficiency of a centrifugal pump by altering the shape of the casing of chamber surrounding the impeller.

A centrifugal pump delivers $30\text{ liters}$ of water per second against a head of $12\text{ m}$ and running at $1500\text{ rpm}$ requires $6\text{ horse power}$. Determine the discharge, head of the pump and power required if the pump runs at $1800\text{ rpm}$.
How the speed of a reaction turbine governed by servo motor, discuss with neat sketch.

Write short notes (any four) on the following:

1. Sonic boom
2. Jet pump
3. Torque converter
4. Muschel curve
5. NPSH
State and deduce the Euler's equation of work done for fluid machinery form the RTT's.
Design a Pelton wheel for the following data: Head of water = 150 m, power to be developed = 600 kW and speed of the wheel = 360 rpm. Assume, reasonably, the missing data.
What is the physical significance of unit quantities on the performance of the hydraulic turbine? Derive the expressions of "unit speed" and "unit power" from the concept of unit turbine.

In a hydroelectric station, water is available at the rate of $175 \text{ m}^3/\text{s}$ under a head of 18 m. If the available turbines run at a speed of 150 rpm with overall efficiency of 82%, determine the number of turbine required, in case of
Francis turbine with maximum specific speed of 460 rpm
Kaplan turbine with maximum specific speed of 350 rpm.
Describe the functions and working principle of air vessels in a reciprocating pump.

The piston of a double acting reciprocating pump has a diameter of 120 cm and a stroke of 60 cm. the pump runs at 20 rpm and discharges through a 15 cm main 76 m long (friction factor, $f = 0.03$), the vertical lift being 46 m. If no air vessel is used, determine the heads in the cylinder at the ends and middle of the stroke.
State is the purpose of a directional control valve? Name the three ways by which a directional control valve may be actuated.
Explain why a pressure relief is used in a hydraulic system.
Draw the general layout of a hydraulic system and also a pneumatic system showing the basic components in them.
For the crane system shown in Fig. Q.4(d), determine the hydraulic cylinder force required to lift a 2000 N load.
Differentiates among compressors, fans and blowers.
Deduce the work done and efficiency equation of fan with help of parametric analysis.

A centrifugal fan running at 1500 rpm has inner and outer diameters of the impeller as 0.2 m and 0.24 m. The absolute and relative velocities of air at entry are 21 m/s and 20 m/s, respectively, and those at exit are 25 m/s and 18 m/s, respectively. The flow rate is 0.6 kg/s, and the motor efficiency is 80%. Determine (i) the stage pressure rise, (ii) the degree of reaction and (iii) the power required to drive the fan. Assume the flow to be incompressible with the density of air as $1.22 \text{ kg/m}^3$.
Explain the effects of blade profile on the working of a centrifugal compressor.
What are the methods adopted to increase the efficiency of the centrifugal pump by altering the shape of the casing of chamber surrounding the impeller? Briefly explain with neat sketches.

A three-stages centrifugal pump has impeller 40 cm in diameter and 2 cm wide. If the blade angle at outlet is $45^\circ$ and the area occupied by the thickness of the vanes may be assumed 8% of the outlet area. If the pump delivers $3.6 \text{ m}^3/\text{min}$ when running at 920 rpm, determine (i) power of the pump, and (ii) Manometric head.
Why is Pelton wheel called an impulse turbine? Derive the equation for the maximum efficiency of a Pelton wheel.

A Kaplan turbine operating under a net head of 20 m developed 36750 kW win an overall efficiency of 86%. The speed ratio is 2 and the flow ratio 0.69. The hub diameter of the wheel is 0.35 times the outside diameter of the wheel. Find the diameter and speed of the turbine.
Starting from the Euler equation, show that the manometric efficiency of a centrifugal pump is a function of tangential velocity, flow velocity and the vane angle at the out let of the impeller.
Discuss with the help of neat sketch, different heads and main components of a centrifugal pump.

Water is transported from tank A to a tank B by a pump. The dimensions of the system are show in Fig. Q.8(c). The total length of the suction pipe and the delivery pipe are 30 m and 20 m, respectively. The total head losses in the suction pipe and the delivery pipe are 1 m and 2 m, respectively. Determine the water power of the pump needs to deliver if the flow rate is 40 liters/sec. Neglect the velocity head.
What are rotodynamic machines? Deduce the Euler's pump-turbine equation.
Differentiate between pressure relief valve and pressure reducing valve.

A pressure relief valve with a poppet area of $650 \text{ mm}^2$ and a spring constant of $450 \text{ kN/m}$ has its spring is initially compressed by $5 \text{ mm}$. The poppet must move by $2.5 \text{ mm}$ from its fully closed position in order to pass full pump flow through the valve. Determine the cracking pressure of the valve, and also the pressure of the valve needed for full pump flow through the valve.
Establish a relationship between the speed of the jet and the speed of the wheel of a Pelton turbine considering maximum efficiency.

A Pelton wheel is working under a head of $250 \text{ m}$ developing $1000 \text{ kW}$ at $300 \text{ rpm}$. The overall efficiency of the turbine is $85\%$ and the coefficient of the nozzle is $0.98$. The velocity ratio is $0.46$; determine the diameter of the wheel and diameter of the jet, and the size of bucket and number of buckets.
What kind of turbine is a Francis turbine? Compare a Francis turbine with a Kaplan turbine.

A Kaplan turbine working under a head of $20 \text{ m}$ develops $12000 \text{ kW}$ shaft power. Outer and hub diameters of the runner are $3.5 \text{ m}$ and $1.75 \text{ m}$. Guide blade angle at extreme edge of runner is $35^\circ$. Hydraulic and overall efficiencies are $88\%$ and $84\%$. If velocity of whirl is $0$ at outlet, determine the runner vane angles at inlet and exit at extreme edge of runner, and the speed of turbine.
Differentiate between fan, blower and compressor.
Compare axial flow compressor with centrifugal flow compressor.

The rotational speed of a two-bladed axial flow fan is $2900 \text{ rpm}$. At the mean radius of $16.5 \text{ cm}$, the rotor blades operate at $C_l = 0.8$ with $C_p = 0.045$. The inlet guide vanes produce a flow angle of $20^\circ$ to the axial direction and the axial velocity through the stage is constant at $20 \text{ m/s}$. For the mean radius, determine the rotor relative flow angles, the stage efficiency, the rotor static pressure increase, and the size of the blade chord needed for this duty.
Derive an expression for the minimum speed for starting a centrifugal pump.
What is priming? Why it is necessary?

A three stage centrifugal pump has impellers $40 \text{ cm}$ in diameter and $2 \text{ cm}$ wide at outlet. The vanes are curved back at the outlet at $45^\circ$ and reduce the circumferential area by $10\%$. The manometric efficiency is $90\%$ and the overall efficiency is $80\%$. Determine the head generated by the pump when running at $1000 \text{ rpm}$, delivering $50 \text{ liters per second}$. What should be the shaft power?
Estimate the percentage of work saved by fitting air vessels in a single acting reciprocating pump and also in a double acting reciprocating pump.

A single acting reciprocating pump has a plunger diameter of $250 \text{ mm}$ and stroke of $450 \text{ mm}$ and it is driven with SHM at $60 \text{ rpm}$. The length and diameter of delivery pipe are $60 \text{ m}$ and $100 \text{ mm}$ respectively. Determine the power saved in overcoming friction in the delivery pipe by fitting an air vessel on the delivery side of the pump. Assume friction factor $= 0.01$.
Explain the working principle of air lift pump with neat sketch.
Write short notes on Hydraulic accumulator and hydraulic Intensifier.

Find the number of pumps required to take water from a deep well under a total head of $156 \text{ m}$. Also the pumps are identical and are running $1000 \text{ rpm}$. The specific speed of each pump is given as $20$ while the rated capacity of each pump is $150 \text{ liters/s}$.
Distinguish between a fluid coupling and a torque converter. With neat sketch describe the working of a torque converter.

A Pelton wheel develops $6500 \text{ kW}$ at $200 \text{ rpm}$ under a head of $220 \text{ m}$ with an overall efficiency of $85\%$. Determine the unit speed, unit discharge and unit power, and the speed, discharge and power when the turbine is working under a head of $140 \text{ m}$.
Define Boyle's law, Charles's law and Gay-Lussac law with their empirical relation.
Define specific heat and write the total heat equation in terms of specific heat and temperature difference.

A certain quantity of air has a volume of $0.4\text{ m}^3$ at a pressure of $500\text{ kPa}$ and a temperature of $80^\circ\text{C}$. It is expanded (i) Isothermally (ii) Isentropically and (iii) Polytropically ($n = 1.5$). Determine the work done by the air during expansion for each case.
Define Compressibility and Bulk modulus of elasticity.

Derive the equation for pressure-height relationship for compressible fluid.

$$2.3\log\left(\frac{P_2}{P_1}\right) = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{RT}$$

A supersonic nozzle is to be designed for air flow with $M = 3$ at the exit section which is $200\text{ mm}$ diameter. The pressure and temperature of air at the nozzle exit are to be $7.85\text{ kN/m}^2$ and $200\text{ K}$ respectively. Determine the reservoir pressure and temperature and throat area. (Take $\gamma = 1.4$).
Explain the influence of impeller blade shape on the performance of centrifugal compressor.
What is surging in axial flow compressor? How do you control a compressor surge?

A three stage compressor delivers air at $70\text{ bar}$ from an atmospheric pressure of $1\text{ bar}$ and $30^\circ\text{C}$. Assuming the intercooling complete, estimate the amount of minimum work required to deal with $1\text{ kg}$ of air. Also find the amount of heat rejected in each intercooler. The index of compression is $1.2$ throughout. Take $c_p$ for air $= 1.005\text{ kJ/kg-K}$.
Discuss briefly with neat sketches Overshot water wheel and Poncelet water wheel.

A breast wheel of $8\text{ m}$ diameter and $2\text{ m}$ width is working under a head of $5\text{ m}$. The depth of shroud is $400\text{ mm}$ and the bucket move with a velocity of $1.5\text{ m/s}$ with $1/2$ full. Calculate the power of the wheel, if its efficiency is $50\%$.
Prove that the hydraulic efficiency of a Pelton wheel turbine is maximum when the jet velocity striking the runner is twice the tangential velocity of the runner.

A Pelton wheel is to be designed for a head of $60\text{ m}$ when running at $200\text{ rpm}$. The Pelton wheel develops $95.647\text{ kW}$ shaft power. The velocity of the buckets is $0.45$ times the velocity of the jet. Overall efficiency is $0.85$ and coefficient of velocity is $0.98$.
What is governing of turbine? Explain with neat sketch, the governing of impulse turbine.

A turbine is operating under a head of $25\text{ m}$ at $200\text{ rpm}$. The discharge is $9\text{ m}^3\text{/s}$. If the efficiency is $90\%$, determine:

Power generated

Specific speed of the turbine, and

Type of turbine.

Discuss the basic working principle of hydraulic press with neat sketches. A hydraulic press has a ram of $500\text{ mm}$ and a plunger of $50\text{ mm}$ diameter. What force is required on the handle to lift a load of $20\text{ kN}$, if the leverage is $1:10$?

It is required to deliver $0.048\text{ m}^3\text{/s}$ of water to a height of $24\text{ m}$ through a $150\text{ mm}$ diameter pipe and $120\text{ m}$ long, by a centrifugal pump. If the overall efficiency of the pump is $75\%$ and coefficient of friction, $f = 0.01$ for the pipe line, find the power required to drive the pump.
Show from first principle that worked saved against friction in the delivery pipe of a double-acting reciprocating pump, by fitting air vessel is $39.2\%$.

A single acting reciprocating pump, running at $50\text{ rpm}$ delivers $0.01\text{ m}^3\text{/s}$ of water. The diameter of the piston is $200\text{ mm}$ and stroke length $400\text{ mm}$. Determine:

The theoretical discharge of the pump.

Co-efficient of discharge.

Slip and percentage slip of the pump.
What is the difference between a hydraulic coupling and a hydraulic torque converter?
